#deck:Japanese Grammar::13 - L1 Interference::Production
The book that I bought
column3: わたしがかったほん
column5: pre-noun clause: 私が買った本 — relative clause precedes noun; no 'that/which'

column1: The article that Tanaka wrote
column3: たなかさんがかいたきじ
column4: 田中さんが書いた記事
column5: pre-noun clause: past action → noun — agent performs action, noun is result/object

column1: The movie that I watched yesterday
column2: 時間詞 + 動詞(過去) + 名詞
column3: きのうみたえいが
column5: pre-noun clause: time + verb → noun — time adverb integrated into pre-nominal clause

column1: The apartment where he lives
column3: かれがすんでいるあぱーと
column4: 彼が住んでいるアパート
column5: pre-noun clause: location verb → noun — location predicate modifies the place-noun

column1: The student that the teacher is teaching
column2: 動詞(現在進行) + 名詞
column3: せんせいがおしえているがくせい
column4: 先生が教えている学生
column5: pre-noun clause: progressive → noun — ongoing state used as pre-nominal description

column1: The person that I know
column2: 状態動詞(現在) + 名詞
column3: わたしがしっているひと
column5: pre-noun clause: state verb → noun — stative predicate precedes the modified noun

column1: The park where children play
column3: こどもたちがあそぶこうえん
column4: 子どもたちが遊ぶ公園
column5: pre-noun clause: habitual action → place — activity identifies the place used for it

column1: The product that the company developed
column3: きぎょうがかいはつしたせいひん
column5: pre-noun clause: development → product — agent's past action qualifies the product

column1: The coffee that I drink every day
column2: 時間詞 + 動詞(現在) + 名詞
column3: まいにちのむこーひー
column5: pre-noun clause: ownership verb → noun — possession relation expressed before noun

column1: The dish that my grandmother made
column3: そぼがつくったりょうり
column5: pre-noun clause: repeated action → person — agent's habitual role modifies noun

column1: The book that my friend recommended
column3: ともだちがすすめたほん
column5: pre-noun clause: goal verb → noun — intended action precedes the modified goal

column1: The problem that we solved
column3: わたしたちがかいけつしたもんだい
column4: 私たちが解決した問題
column5: pre-noun clause: production verb → noun — creation process precedes the noun

column1: The computer that Hanako is using
column2: 動詞(現在進行) + 名詞
column3: はなこがつかっているぱそこん
column4: 花子が使っているパソコン
column5: pre-noun clause: caused state → noun — caused condition precedes affected thing

column1: The service that the company provides
column3: かいしゃがていきょうするさーびす
column4: 会社が提供するサービス
column5: pre-noun clause: potential → noun — ability predicate modifies the potential actor

column1: The martial art that I study
column2: 動詞(現在進行) + 名詞
column3: わたしがならっているぶどう
column5: pre-noun clause: future action → noun — upcoming activity qualifies the noun

column1: The building that was built last year
column2: 時間詞 + 受身動詞(過去) + 名詞
column3: さくねんたてられたたてもの
column5: pre-noun clause: joint activity → noun — shared doing precedes the shared thing

column1: The career path that my parents decided
column3: おやがきめたしんろ
column5: pre-noun clause: reception verb → person — recipient of action precedes person-noun

column1: The symptom that the patient reported
column3: かんじゃさんがうったえたしょうじょう
column4: 患者さんが訴えた症状
column5: pre-noun clause: past state → noun — prior condition qualifies the current noun

column1: The technology that the world needs
column2: 動詞(現在進行) + 名詞
column3: せかいがひつようとしているぎじゅつ
column4: 世界が必要としている技術
column5: pre-noun clause: causation → result — cause-effect chain compresses into pre-noun

column1: The book that I am reading
column2: 動詞(現在進行) + 名詞
column3: わたしがよんでいるほん
column5: pre-noun clause: sensory verb → noun — perceived thing precedes the perceiver

column1: The tools that my grandfather loved using
column2: 動詞(過去進行) + 名詞
column3: そふがあいようしていたこうぐ
column4: 祖父が愛用していた工具
column5: pre-noun clause: comparative → noun — comparison clause precedes the compared item

column1: The budget that the CEO approved
column3: しゃちょうがしょうにんしたよさん
column5: pre-noun clause: experience → noun — experiential clause precedes experiencer

column1: The medicine that the doctor prescribed
column3: いしゃがしょほうしたくすり
column5: pre-noun clause: temporal → noun — when-clause collapses into pre-nominal modifier

column1: The goal that we achieved
column3: わたしたちがたっせいしたもくひょう
column4: 私たちが達成した目標
column5: pre-noun clause: manner → noun — how-clause reduces to pre-noun description

column1: The present that she wants
column3: かのじょがほしいぷれぜんと
column4: 彼女が欲しいプレゼント
column5: pre-noun clause: complex stacking — multiple pre-noun clauses chain before one noun
